page: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes a substantial local storage capacity of 2835 GB and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in compute-intensive and data-processing tasks. The Intel Xeon processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to a Passmark CPU score of 66,237.87 and an extended instructions score of 59,749.83. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier performance levels at 36.36 GB/sec and 14.96 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 302.08 GB/sec. For memory-bound operations, the system achieves a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 3,537.72 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write speeds are 174.64 GB/sec and 69.06 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the D48lds_v7 offers high compute density and local storage capacity, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require raw processing power and fast local scratch space rather than massive memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per core, which may limit its suitability for memory-intensive in-memory databases. However, it demonstrates strong performance in database operations with a score of 22,353.15 on Passmark and Redis SET operations at 79.57 million operations per second. It is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads, web serving with 75.8 GB/sec throughput, and small-scale LLM inference, where it achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 338.79 tokens per second for a 135M model.
description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Featuring 96.0 GB of RAM and 2835 GB of local storage, this instance is designed for high-compute workloads requiring fast local disk access. It lacks GPU acceleration but delivers strong performance across various benchmarks, including a Passmark CPU score of 66,237.87 and a static web serving throughput of 75.8 GB/sec. The system's low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications, but its support for AVX-512 and AMX instructions enables top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation. It is highly suited for web serving, database operations, and compute-intensive data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering strong performance for web serving and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 features 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads and web serving.
tagline: A high-compute, low-memory virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and substantial local storage for demanding data workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory and 2835 GB of fast local storage
Top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation speeds
Strong performance in database operations and static web serving
Optimized for compute-heavy tasks, web hosting, and data processing
